unicode.value: Realización de las diversas actividades de la tarea 3 del curso "de METODOLOGÍAS INNOVADORAS PARA LA FP A DISTANCIA MEDIANTE EXELEARNING (261105FP118)"
Enter the text that will appear on this iDevice
Situation is an iDevice that provides the student with a story that will guide her along the learning process.
Enter the text that will appear on this iDevice

Estás escribiendo tu primer programa en C++. Al darle al botón "Ejecutar", el IDE (entorno integrado de desarrollo) te muestra una lista de errores que no entiendes. Una compañera te dice: "Es que el código fuente no es lo que el ordenador lee directamente, primero hay que pasar por el compilador".

¿Qué crees que hace exactamente ese "compilador" con el texto que has escrito?

The majority of a learning resource will be establishing context, delivering instructions and providing general information. This provides the framework within which the learning activities are built and delivered.
Use this field to enter text. This iDevice has no emphasis applied although limited formatting can be applied to text through the text editing buttons associated with the field.

La compilación es el proceso de traducción de un código fuente (escrito en un lenguaje de alto nivel, como C, Java o C#) a un código de bajo nivel o código máquina que el procesador pueda ejecutar. Sin este proceso, el hardware sería incapaz de interpretar nuestras instrucciones.

  • Editor de texto
  • Código fuente
  • Compilador
  • Código objeto/máquina
Enter a question for learners to reflect upon.

Reflection is a teaching method often used to connect theory to practice. Reflection tasks often provide learners with an opportunity to observe and reflect on their observations before presenting these as a piece of academic work. Journals, diaries, profiles and portfolios are useful tools for collecting observation data. Rubrics and guides can be effective feedback tools.
Enter a question for learners to reflect upon.

¿Sería posible que un ordenador ejecutara directamente un archivo .cpp o .java sin ningún software de por medio? Piensa en la diferencia entre el lenguaje humano y el binario.

Todo el mundo en este país debería aprender a programar un ordenador, porque eso te enseña a pensar.

Steve Jobs

The majority of a learning resource will be establishing context, delivering instructions and providing general information. This provides the framework within which the learning activities are built and delivered.
Use this field to enter text. This iDevice has no emphasis applied although limited formatting can be applied to text through the text editing buttons associated with the field.

Antes de generar el archivo final, el compilador divide su trabajo en fases. La primera es el análisis, donde se comprueba que el código esté bien escrito, no falten puntos y coma, y las variables existan.

El compilador debe realizar 3 tipos de análisis (ALX, AST y ASM) antes de pasar a la siguiente fase de síntesis donde, de no existir errores, obtenemos el código máquina u objeto.

Tipos de análisis
Análisis Características
Lexicográfico Comprueba que el código fuente está escrito correctamente.
Sintáctico Verifica que los elementos obtenidos en el ALX se relacionan respetando la sintaxis del lenguaje de programación utilizado.
Semántico Asegura que el código fuente, además de estar bien escrito y respetar las reglas de sintaxis del lenguaje usado, tiene sentido.
Enter the text that will appear on this iDevice
Highlighted is an iDevice that emphasizes text to catch the student's attention.
Enter the text that will appear on this iDevice

¡Importante!

El análisis léxicográfico y sintáctico detecta errores de escritura, pero no asegura que el programa haga lo que tú quieres (lógica).

El análisis semántico detecta situaciones de inconsistencia tal como sucede con este texto: Los elefantes bebían neumáticos. Todo, aparentemente, es correcto, tanto lexicográfica como sintácticamente, pero nos chirría porque falla la semántica (los neumáticos no tienen el atributo o cualidad de ser bebibles).
Enter the available choices here. You can add options by clicking the "Add Another Option" button. Delete options by clicking the red "X" next to the Option.

Type in the feedback that you want the student to see when selecting the particular option. If you don't complete this box, eXe will automatically provide default feedback as follows: "Correct answer" as indicated by the selection for the correct answer; or "Wrong answer" for the other options.
Enter a hint here. If you do not want to provide a hint, leave this field blank.
Select the correct option by clicking on the radio button.

Although more often used in formal testing situations MCQs can be used as a testing tool to stimulate thought and discussion on topics students may feel a little reticent in responding to. When designing a MCQ test consider the following:
  • What learning outcomes are the questions testing
  • What intellectual skills are being tested
  • What are the language skills of the audience
  • Gender and cultural issues
  • Avoid grammar language and question structures that might provide clues
Enter the question stem. The quest should be clear and unambiguous. Avoid negative premises as these can tend to be ambiguous.

When building an MCQ consider the following:
  • Use phrases that learners are familiar with and have encountered in their study
  • Keep responses concise
  • There should be some consistency between the stem and the responses
  • Provide enough options to challenge learners to think about their response
  • Try to make sure that correct responses are not more detailed than the distractors
  • Distractors should be incorrect but plausible
Enter the question stem. The quest should be clear and unambiguous. Avoid negative premises as these can tend to be ambiguous.
¿Cómo se llama el error que ocurre cuando te olvidas de cerrar una llave { en el código?
Enter a hint here. If you do not want to provide a hint, leave this field blank.
Tiene que ver con lo que aprendiste y practicaste en la asignatura de Lengua.
Enter the available choices here. You can add options by clicking the "Add Another Option" button. Delete options by clicking the red "X" next to the Option.

Type in the feedback that you want the student to see when selecting the particular option. If you don't complete this box, eXe will automatically provide default feedback as follows: "Correct answer" as indicated by the selection for the correct answer; or "Wrong answer" for the other options.
Enter the available choices here. You can add options by clicking the "Add Another Option" button. Delete options by clicking the red "X" next to the Option.

Type in the feedback that you want the student to see when selecting the particular option. If you don't complete this box, eXe will automatically provide default feedback as follows: "Correct answer" as indicated by the selection for the correct answer; or "Wrong answer" for the other options.
Enter the available choices here. You can add options by clicking the "Add Another Option" button. Delete options by clicking the red "X" next to the Option.

Type in the feedback that you want the student to see when selecting the particular option. If you don't complete this box, eXe will automatically provide default feedback as follows: "Correct answer" as indicated by the selection for the correct answer; or "Wrong answer" for the other options.

The majority of a learning resource will be establishing context, delivering instructions and providing general information. This provides the framework within which the learning activities are built and delivered.
Use this field to enter text. This iDevice has no emphasis applied although limited formatting can be applied to text through the text editing buttons associated with the field.

Una vez que el código es correcto, el compilador genera un código intermedio o directamente el código objeto. Aquí entran en juego los Optimizadores, que intentan que el programa resultante sea lo más rápido y ligero posible.
Enter the text that will appear on this iDevice
A Step Ahead is an iDevice that permits students widen their knowledge with further contents.
Enter the text that will appear on this iDevice

Existen lenguajes que no se compilan "del todo" antes de ejecutarse, sino que usan una máquina virtual (como Java) o son interpretados (como Python). Investiga el concepto de Just-In-Time compilation (JIT).

Lenguajes interpretados y JIT
Descripción del vídeo

Although more often used in formal testing situations MCQs can be used as a testing tool to stimulate thought and discussion on topics students may feel a little reticent in responding to. When designing a MCQ test consider the following:
  • What learning outcomes are the questions testing
  • What intellectual skills are being tested
  • What are the language skills of the audience
  • Gender and cultural issues
  • Avoid grammar language and question structures that might provide clues

When building an MCQ consider the following:
  • Use phrases that learners are familiar with and have encountered in their study
  • Keep responses concise
  • There should be some consistency between the stem and the responses
  • Provide enough options to challenge learners to think about their response
  • Try to make sure that correct responses are not more detailed than the distractors
  • Distractors should be incorrect but plausible
Introduzca la pregunta. La pregunta debe ser clara y no ambigua. Evite las premisas negativas ya que estas tienden a confundir a los estudiantes.
Selecciona los elementos que suelen generarse tras una compilación exitosa:
Select as many correct answer options as required by clicking the check box beside the option.
Enter the available choices here. You can add options by clicking the "Add another option" button. Delete options by clicking the red X next to the option.
Enter the question stem. The question should be clear and unambiguous. Avoid negative premises as these can tend to confuse learners.
Type in the feedback you want to provide the learner with.
Type in the feedback you want to provide the learner with.
Enter the available choices here. You can add options by clicking the "Add another option" button. Delete options by clicking the red X next to the option.
Enter the available choices here. You can add options by clicking the "Add another option" button. Delete options by clicking the red X next to the option.
Enter the available choices here. You can add options by clicking the "Add another option" button. Delete options by clicking the red X next to the option.

The majority of a learning resource will be establishing context, delivering instructions and providing general information. This provides the framework within which the learning activities are built and delivered.
Use this field to enter text. This iDevice has no emphasis applied although limited formatting can be applied to text through the text editing buttons associated with the field.

Para compilar en entornos reales de FP, solemos usar herramientas como gcc (para C), javac (para Java) o los botones integrados en IDEs como Visual Studio Code o Eclipse. Es vital leer el output de la consola para depurar.
Enter the text that will appear on this iDevice
A Piece of Advice is an iDevice that permits the teacher give advice on interesting books, films...
Enter the text that will appear on this iDevice
Te recomendamos que siempre compiles tu código de forma incremental. No escribas 200 líneas de golpe; compila cada vez que añadas una funcionalidad nueva para localizar errores fácilmente.
Enter the text that will appear on this iDevice
You Should Know is an iDevice that helps the student widen her knowledge with core contents.
Enter the text that will appear on this iDevice
Linker (Enlazador): es la herramienta que une tu código compilado con las librerías del sistema para crear el ejecutable final. Sin el enlazador, el proceso de compilación estaría incompleto.
